lines: Before you reach for your phone. Before you check the time. Before the day begins... be here. Just five minutes.
Take a deep breath in through your nose... and at the top, sip in a little more air... now let it all go through your mouth. A long, slow exhale.
Again. Deep inhale... sip more in at the top... and release. Feel your shoulders drop as the air leaves.
One more time. Inhale... sip... and let everything go.

Good. You're here. Feel your body in this moment. The weight of your head on the pillow, or your feet on the floor, or wherever you are right now. Notice the temperature of the air on your skin. The sounds in the room or beyond it. You don't have to change anything. Just arrive.

Now notice your chest. Place a hand there if you'd like. Feel it rise and fall. This rhythm has been keeping you alive all night while you slept. You didn't manage it. You didn't earn it. It was given.
There's a verse in Lamentations... a book written in the middle of grief and exile, when everything had fallen apart... and even there, in that rubble, the writer finds this:
"The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning. Great is his faithfulness."
New every morning. Not recycled. Not warmed over. Not the leftovers of yesterday's grace. New.

Whatever yesterday held... the failures, the frustrations, the conversations you wish you could redo, the things left unfinished, the moments you weren't the person you wanted to be... this morning's mercy was made for today. It arrived before you did. It was here waiting when your eyes opened.

Sit with that for a moment. You didn't earn this morning. You didn't qualify for it. It was given. And the love behind it... is steadfast. It doesn't waver based on your performance. It doesn't fluctuate with your mood or your productivity. It is steady. It is faithful. It is here.
Now, before planning, before problem-solving... before the mind starts running its lists... let's name what's already good. Three things you're grateful for this morning.
The first one comes easily. Something obvious, right in front of you. A person, a place, a simple comfort. Name it quietly.

The second takes a little more looking. Something small, maybe. Something you'd normally walk right past without a second thought. The ability to take a full breath. Hot water. A familiar voice. Name it.

The third... dig a little deeper. Something you've been taking for granted. A person who shows up for you. A capacity you forget isn't guaranteed. A mercy you stopped noticing because it's been so constant. Name it.

Let those three things rest in your chest for a moment. Gratitude isn't just a thought... it's a felt sense. Research shows that when you name what you're thankful for, your body actually responds... your heart rate settles, your breathing slows, cortisol drops. So let it land. Not just in your mind. In your body.

Lord... thank you. For this morning I didn't earn. For mercies I didn't ask for. For the three gifts I just named, and the hundreds I'll never notice. For steadfast love that met me before I was even awake. Open my eyes a little wider today. Help me see the new mercies as they come... not just at the end of the day looking back, but in the moment, as they arrive. Amen.
Now... one word for today. Not a to-do list. Not a strategy. One quality you want to carry with you through whatever comes.
Patience. Courage. Presence. Kindness. Focus. Joy. Gentleness. Steadiness.
What does today need from you? What kind of person do you want to be in the rooms you'll walk into?

Hold that word. Let it settle into the center of your chest... like a warm coal that will glow quietly through the hours ahead. Not something you have to grip tightly. Something you carry lightly, and return to when you drift.

Now picture yourself walking into the first moment of your day... carrying that word. See yourself at your desk, or in the car, or at the kitchen table with your family... and that intention is still with you. Not as pressure. Not as another thing to perform. But as direction. A quiet compass pointing you back to who you want to be.
See yourself in the middle of the afternoon, when the morning's clarity has faded... and you remember. The word. The gratitude. The mercy that was new this morning and is still new right now.

Today will have challenges. You don't know all of them yet, and that's okay. You step into this day carrying gratitude and intention, held by a love that never ceases and mercies that are already... new.
May God's steadfast love go with you this morning. And may his new mercies meet you in every moment you forgot to expect them.
